seo.title: Building Customer Support with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with LangChain enables powerful RAG pipelines and tool-calling for CRM integration and ticket management. However, hallucinations in retrieval, infinite tool loops, context overflows, and inconsistent formatting can lead to poor customer experiences and compliance violations. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, PII safety, tone consistency, and policy adherence in high-risk support scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangChain customer support agent combines RAG for knowledge base retrieval with agent executors for CRM tools like ticket creation and order lookup. User queries trigger vector store retrieval from support docs, followed by an LLM agent that decides between direct responses, tool calls (e.g., check_order_status, create_ticket), or escalation. Chains handle conversation history to maintain context across multi-turn interactions, with output parsers ensuring structured responses.

The architecture flows from user input → retriever → agent executor → tools/LLM → verified response. CRM integrations use custom tools wrapping APIs like Salesforce or Zendesk, while knowledge bases use FAISS or Pinecone for fast similarity search. Long conversations risk context overflow, requiring summarization chains.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent fabricates order details or policy info when retrieval misses relevant docs
risks.mitigation: Wrap chain.invoke() with vex.verify() using response accuracy checks against ground truth

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agent repeatedly calls order_lookup without progressing conversation
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors tool call sequences and enforces max iterations per turn

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Token limits cause truncated history, losing conversation context
risks.mitigation: Vex truncates/summarizes history and verifies context relevance before LLM calls

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Mixed JSON/text responses break frontend parsers and ticket creation
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces structured output schemas with Pydantic/JSON schema validation

implementation.title: RAG Chain for Knowledge Base
implementation.description: Basic retrieval chain augmented with conversation history for support queries
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from langchain.embeddings import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain.llms import OpenAI

# Setup retriever
embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()
vectorstore = FAISS.load_local('support_kb', embeddings)
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

llm = OpenAI(model='gpt-4')
qa_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(
    llm=llm,
    chain_type='stuff',
    retriever=retriever,
    return_source_documents=True
)

# Customer support query
result = qa_chain.invoke({
    'query': 'How do I reset my password?',
    'chat_history': []  # From session store
})
print(result['result'])

implementation.title: CRM Tool for Order Lookup
implementation.description: Custom tool integrates with mock CRM API for order status

implementation.title: Agent Executor Setup
implementation.description: ReAct agent with support tools and memory
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferMemory

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key='chat_history', return_messages=True)

tools = [order_tool, create_ticket_tool]  # From previous examples
agent = initialize_agent(
    tools,
    llm,
    agent=AgentType.CONVERSATIONAL_REACT_DESCRIPTION,
    memory=memory,
    verbose=True,
    max_iterations=5  # Prevent loops
)

response = agent.run('My order 12345 is late, what\'s the status?')
print(response)

implementation.title: TypeScript RAG Chain

vexIntegration.title: Verify Response Accuracy & PII
vexIntegration.description: Wrap agent execution with comprehensive verification for support responses
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.verify(
    checks=[
        'response_accuracy',  # Against retrieved docs
        'pii_filtering',      # No customer data leakage
        'tone_consistency'    # Professional support tone
    ],
    risk_profile='high'
)
def verified_support_agent(query: str, history: list) -> str:
    result = agent.run(query)
    return result

# Usage
safe_response = verified_support_agent(
    'My order 12345 is late',
    session_history
)

# Vex auto-blocks: hallucinated facts, PII, rude tone
